name: We were burned by a previous AI/LLMO vendor—how should we think about trying LLMO strategy again?
description: Use the prior bad experience to sharpen how you scope and evaluate LLMO work, not as a reason to abandon it. Start with a small, explicit audit focused on promp

mainEntity.name: We were burned by a previous AI/LLMO vendor—how should we think about trying LLMO strategy again?

acceptedAnswer.text:
If you already had a bad experience with an “AI marketing” vendor, treat that as a signal to tighten your evaluation criteria, not to abandon LLMO altogether. The discipline itself is valuable; the execution might have been poor.

Often, the failure stems from vague goals, no measurement, or treating LLMO as a one-off campaign.

Key factors:
- Whether your previous vendor defined specific prompts and metrics upfront.
- How transparent they were about limitations and timeframes.
- Whether they focused only on content volume instead of evidence quality.
- How much reusable insight or infrastructure they left behind.

Re-start with a small, clearly scoped LLMO audit and insist on explicit deliverables: a prompt set, visibility benchmark, and prioritized action list. Choose partners willing to challenge assumptions and show you their methodology in detail, not just their case studies.
acceptedAnswer.description: Use the prior bad experience to sharpen how you scope and evaluate LLMO work, not as a reason to abandon it. Start with a small, explicit audit focused on prompts and measurement, and only work with partners who can show a clear methodology and realistic expectations.
